book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
To Edilbert, King of the Angli.

ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To Edilbert, King of the Angli.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Edilbert, &c.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: On this account Almighty God advances good men to the government of peoples, that through them He may bestow the gifts of His loving-kindness on all over whom they are preferred.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: This we have found to be the case in the nation of the Angli, which your Glory has been put over to the intent that through the good things granted to you, heavenly benefits might be conferred on the nation subject to you.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And so, glorious son, keep guard with anxious mind over the grace which thou hast received from above.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Make haste to extend the Christian faith among the peoples under thy sway, redouble the zeal of thy rectitude in their conversion, put down the worship of idols, overturn the edifices of their temples, build up the manners of thy subjects in great purity of life by exhorting, by terrifying, by enticing, by correcting, by shewing examples of well-doing; that so you may find Him your recompenser in heaven Whose name and knowledge you shall have spread abroad on earth.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For He Himself will make the name of your glory even more glorious to posterity, if you seek and maintain His honour among the nations.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For so Constantine, the once most pious Emperor, recalling the Roman republic from perverse worshippings of idols, subjected it with himself to our Almighty Lord God Jesus Christ, and turned himself with his subject peoples with all his heart to Him.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Hence it came to pass that that man surpassed in praise the name of ancient princes, and excelled his predecessors as much in renown as in well-doing.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And now, therefore, let your Glory make haste to infuse into the kings and peoples subject to you the knowledge of God, Father, Son and Holy Spirit, that you may both surpass the ancient kings of your race in renown and in deserts, and the more you shall have wiped away the sins of others among your subjects, the more secure you may become with regard to your own sins before the terrible scrutiny of Almighty God.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Moreover, you have with you our most reverend brother, Augustine the bishop, learned in monastic rule, replete with knowledge of holy Scripture, endowed by the grace of God with good works.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Listen gladly to his admonitions, follow them devoutly, keep them studiously in remembrance: for, if you listen to him in what he speaks in behalf of Almighty God, the same Almighty God will the sooner listen to him when he prays for you.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For, if (which God forbid) you disregard his words, when will it be possible for Almighty God to hear him for you, whom you neglect to hear for God?

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: With all your heart, therefore, bind ye yourselves in fervour of faith to him, and aid his endeavours by the power which he gives you from above, that He Whose faith you cause to be received and kept in your kingdom may Himself make you partakers of His own Kingdom.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Furthermore, we would have your Glory know that, as we learn from the words of the Almighty Lord in holy Scripture, the end of the present world is already close at hand, and the reign of the saints is coming, which can have no end.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, now that this end of the world is approaching, many things are at hand which previously have not been; to wit, changes of the air, terrors from heaven, and seasons contrary to the accustomed order of times, wars, famine, pestilences, earthquakes in divers places.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Yet these things will not come in our days, but after our days they will all ensue.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: You therefore, if you observe any of these things occurring in your land, by no means let your mind be troubled, since these signs of the end of the world are sent beforehand for this purpose, that we should be solicitous about our souls, suspectful of the hour of death, and in our good deeds be found prepared for the coming Judge.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: These things, glorious son, we have now briefly spoken of, that, when the Christian faith shall have been extended in your kingdom, our speech to you may also extend itself to greater length, and that we may be pleased to speak so much the more fully as joy multiplies itself in our heart for the perfected conversion of your nation.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I have sent you some small presents, which to you will not be small, when received by you as of the benediction of the blessed Apostle Peter.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And so may Almighty God guard and perfect in you the grace which He has begun, and extend your life here through courses of many years, and after a long life receive you in the congregation of the heavenly country.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: May heavenly grace keep your Excellency safe, sir son (domine fili).

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Given this 10th day of the Kalends of July, the 19th year of the empire of our most pious lord Mauricius Tiberius Augustus, the 18th year after the consulship of the same our lord, Indiction 4.
